differences.title: 1. REACTIVITY MODEL
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS uses signals for direct DOM updates, offering performance benefits.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik optimizes initial load by pausing execution and resuming on user interaction.

differences.title: 2. INITIAL LOAD
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS has zero hydration overhead, directly rendering components.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik aims for near-zero hydration with its resumability approach.

differences.title: 3. COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS compiles JSX to efficient vanilla JS and DOM operations.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik serializes component state and logic for efficient resuming.

differences.title: 4. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Primarily uses signals, providing granular control and performance.
differences.rightDesc: Leverages context and stores, designed to be resumable.

differences.title: 5. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Excellent runtime performance due to fine-grained reactivity.
differences.rightDesc: Superior initial load times and interactivity.

differences.title: 6. ECOSYSTEM SIZE
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS has a strong and active community with growing library support.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik is newer, its ecosystem is still developing.

differences.title: 7. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Easier transition for those familiar with reactive patterns.
differences.rightDesc: Requires understanding resumability and its implications.
